game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
{
  "original_game_name": "Archero 2",
  "game_name": "Dungeon Dasher",
  "genre": "Top-down Roguelike Action",
  "core_mechanics": "The game operates on a discrete grid-based movement system within procedurally generated rooms. The player character (PC) moves one grid cell per directional input. While stationary (no movement keys pressed), the PC automatically attacks the nearest enemy within a predefined range by firing a projectile. This attack ceases immediately upon movement input. Each entity (PC, enemies) has health points (HP), attack damage (ATK), attack speed (ASP), and movement speed (SPD). Projectiles have their own speed and damage. Collisions are handled for player-to-enemy (no direct collision damage but can block movement or push in some cases for specific enemy types, primarily for positional interaction), projectile-to-enemy, and projectile-to-player. When a room is cleared of all enemies, the game transitions to an upgrade selection state where the player is presented with three randomly chosen power-ups (e.g., +ATK, +HP, +SPD, multi-shot, piercing projectiles, increased projectile speed). The player selects one, and the chosen upgrade applies for the remainder of the current run. The game progresses through a series of rooms, grouped into levels, each presenting increasing difficulty. State tracking includes: PC position (x, y), HP, ATK, ASP, SPD, current upgrades, current room ID, current level, list of active enemies (type, position, HP), list of active player projectiles, list of active enemy projectiles, game state (menu, playing, paused, etc.), and current score.",
  "visual_elements": "All visuals are rendered using p5.js drawing functions.

**Game Objects:**
-   **Player Character (PC):** A blue-filled square, 40x40 pixels. When hit, it flashes white briefly.
-   **Basic Melee Enemy:** A red-filled circle, 40x40 pixels. Moves towards the player. When hit, flashes white.
-   **Ranged Enemy:** A green-filled triangle, 40x40 pixels. Moves towards player, stops to fire projectiles. When hit, flashes white.
-   **Tank Enemy:** A purple-filled larger square, 60x60 pixels. Slower, higher HP, higher damage melee. When hit, flashes white.
-   **Boss Enemy:** A larger, darker purple-filled octagon, 80x80 pixels. Unique visual for the final boss. When hit, flashes white.
-   **Player Projectile:** A small yellow-filled circle, 10x10 pixels. Travels in a straight line from the player towards the targeted enemy.
-   **Enemy Projectile:** A small orange-filled square, 10x10 pixels. Travels in a straight line from the enemy towards the player.
-   **Walls/Obstacles:** Dark grey-filled rectangles or squares, acting as room boundaries and impassable terrain.
-   **Floor:** A dark grey background that fills the playable area of the room.
-   **Health Bars:** A thin green rectangle drawn above each active entity (player and enemies) indicating remaining HP, overlaid on a red background representing max HP.
-   **Upgrade Selection UI:** Three distinct light-blue rectangular panels, each containing text and potentially a simple icon describing the upgrade. A yellow border highlights the currently selected option.

**Animations/Visual Effects:**
-   **Hit Flash:** A brief (e.g., 100ms) change in entity color to white or a lighter shade upon taking damage.
-   **Projectile Travel:** Smooth linear motion of projectile shapes.
-   **Entity Death:** A quick fade-out effect combined with a slight scaling down upon an entity's HP reaching zero.
-   **Room Transition:** A brief fade-to-black effect, followed by a fade-in to the new room layout.
-   **Upgrade Selection:** Hovering over an upgrade option with the mouse or navigating with keys will highlight it with a distinct border or background change.",
  "control_scheme": "The game uses standard keyboard controls for movement and interaction:
-   **ArrowUp (↑):** Move player character one grid cell upwards.
-   **ArrowDown (↓):** Move player character one grid cell downwards.
-   **ArrowLeft (←):** Move player character one grid cell leftwards.
-   **ArrowRight (→):** Move player character one grid cell rightwards.
-   **Spacebar:** In the 'UPGRADE_SELECTION' state, this key confirms the selection of the currently highlighted upgrade. In the 'MAIN_MENU' state, it starts the game. In 'PAUSED' state, it resumes the game.
-   **Z Key:** Toggles the 'PAUSED' state. Pressing Z during gameplay will pause the game and bring up a pause menu. Pressing Z again (or Spacebar) will resume.",
  "game_states": "The game transitions between several distinct states to manage flow:
-   **MAIN_MENU:** The initial state. Displays the game title, a 'Start Game' button (or 'Press Space to Start'), and the high score. Player transitions to 'PLAYING' (Level 1, Room 1) upon selecting 'Start Game'.
-   **PLAYING:** The active gameplay loop. Player controls movement, enemies spawn and attack. The state tracks entity updates, physics, rendering, and collision detection. Transitions to 'UPGRADE_SELECTION' when a room is cleared or 'PAUSED' if the Z key is pressed. Transitions to 'GAME_OVER' if player HP reaches zero.
-   **PAUSED:** Game logic is frozen, but UI elements are rendered. A pause menu with options like 'Resume' (transitions to 'PLAYING') and 'Main Menu' (transitions to 'MAIN_MENU') is displayed. Triggered by pressing 'Z' during 'PLAYING' state.
-   **UPGRADE_SELECTION:** Triggered when all enemies in a room are defeated. Game logic is frozen. Three random upgrades are presented. Player selects one using Spacebar. Transitions back to 'PLAYING' (next room) after selection. If it's the last room of a level, it first transitions to 'LEVEL_TRANSITION'.
-   **LEVEL_TRANSITION:** A brief intermediate state (e.g., 2-3 seconds) after clearing the last room of a level, but before the upgrade selection. Displays 'Level X Complete! Preparing for Level Y...'. Automatically transitions to 'UPGRADE_SELECTION' or 'GAME_WON' if all levels are completed.
-   **GAME_OVER:** Triggered when player HP drops to zero. Displays 'GAME OVER', the final score, and options to 'Retry' (starts new game from Level 1, Room 1) or 'Main Menu' (transitions to 'MAIN_MENU').
-   **GAME_WON:** Triggered when the player successfully clears the final room of the final level. Displays 'YOU WIN!', the final score, and options to 'Main Menu' (transitions to 'MAIN_MENU').",
  "win_conditions": "The player wins by successfully clearing all rooms in the final Level (Level 5). This includes defeating the boss enemy in the final room of Level 5.",
  "lose_conditions": "The player loses if their Health Points (HP) drop to 0 or below at any point during gameplay.",
  "scoring_system": "The game implements a scoring system to track player performance and encourage replayability.
-   **Points Awarded:**
    -   +50 points for defeating a Basic Melee Enemy.
    -   +100 points for defeating a Ranged Enemy.
    -   +150 points for defeating a Tank Enemy.
    -   +500 points for defeating the Boss Enemy in Level 5.
    -   +50 points for clearing any regular room (defeating all enemies within it).
    -   +1000 points for completing an entire Level (clearing all rooms within that level).
-   **Bonus Points/Multipliers:** No additional bonus points or multipliers are implemented for simplicity.
-   **High Score System:** A high score system will be implemented using `localStorage` to persist the highest score achieved across browser sessions. This high score is displayed on the 'MAIN_MENU' screen.
-   **Display:** The current score will be displayed prominently in the top-right corner of the game canvas during gameplay, formatted as: `SCORE: [Current Score]`. The font will be white, size 24px.
-   **Gameplay Impact:** The score does not directly affect gameplay. It is purely for player achievement tracking and competition for the high score.",
  "level_system": "The game features 5 progressively difficult levels, each comprising multiple rooms. Difficulty increases through enemy count, enemy types, enemy stats, and room complexity.

-   **Level 1: The Training Grounds**
    -   **Rooms:** 3 rooms.
    -   **Difficulty:** Introductory.
    -   **Enemies:** Primarily Basic Melee Enemies (low HP, slow movement, low damage). Max 2-3 enemies per room.
    -   **Obstacles:** Very simple, sparse environmental blocks in room layouts.
    -   **Progression:** Clear all 3 rooms, then transition to 'Level 2: The Hive'.

-   **Level 2: The Hive**
    -   **Rooms:** 4 rooms.
    -   **Difficulty:** Moderate.
    -   **Enemies:** Introduction of Ranged Enemies (medium HP, medium movement, fires single projectiles) alongside Basic Melee. Max 3-4 enemies per room, mixed types.
    -   **Obstacles:** More varied obstacle layouts, including some choke points.
    -   **Progression:** Clear all 4 rooms, then transition to 'Level 3: The Depths'.

-   **Level 3: The Depths**
    -   **Rooms:** 5 rooms.
    -   **Difficulty:** Challenging.
    -   **Enemies:** Introduction of Tank Enemies (high HP, slow movement, high melee damage) in addition to Basic Melee and Ranged. Enemies are faster and deal more damage. Max 4-5 enemies per room, denser groupings.
    -   **Obstacles:** Complex layouts with more narrow passages and larger, strategically placed blocks requiring more tactical movement.
    -   **Progression:** Clear all 5 rooms, then transition to 'Level 4: The Arena'.

-   **Level 4: The Arena**
    -   **Rooms:** 5 rooms.
    -   **Difficulty:** Hard.
    -   **Enemies:** All previous enemy types, with increased stats (HP, damage, speed). Introduction of 'Elite' versions (visually distinct, e.g., slightly larger/darker) of existing enemies. Max 6-7 enemies per room, very dense encounters.
    -   **Obstacles:** Very challenging static layouts, and introduction of simple environmental hazards (e.g., small, periodically damaging floor tiles or slow-moving block obstacles for visual flair).
    -   **Progression:** Clear all 5 rooms, then transition to 'Level 5: The Core'.

-   **Level 5: The Core**
    -   **Rooms:** 3 rooms.
    -   **Difficulty:** Very Hard (Boss Level).
    -   **Enemies:** All Elite enemy types, significantly buffed stats across the board. The final room features a unique Boss Enemy with high HP and a distinctive attack pattern (e.g., periodic multi-directional projectiles or a charging attack). Multiple regular enemies also spawn during the boss fight.
    -   **Obstacles:** Highly strategic layouts, often forming a central arena for the boss fight in the final room.
    -   **Progression:** Clear all 3 rooms (including defeating the boss), then trigger the 'GAME_WON' state.

**Level Advancement Display:** When transitioning between levels, a 'LEVEL_TRANSITION' state is triggered, displaying a message like "Level [Current Level] Complete! Preparing for Level [Next Level]..." centered on the screen for 2-3 seconds.
**Level Indicator:** The current level number is displayed in the top-left corner of the game canvas during gameplay, formatted as: `LEVEL: [Current Level]`. The font will be white, size 24px.",
  "simplifications": "The following features from the original Archero 2 concept have been simplified or removed to fit the HTML5 implementation specifications for a focused roguelike experience:
-   **Movement System:** The original virtual joystick for free movement is replaced with a simplified grid-based keyboard control system (Arrow keys).
-   **Procedural Generation Complexity:** While rooms are still concepted as procedurally generated, for implementation, this will be simplified. Instead of truly dynamic generation, room layouts will either be chosen from a pool of pre-designed templates per level, or utilize a basic programmatic generation that places walls and obstacles within a fixed grid structure with limited variability.
-   **Enemy Attack Patterns:** Complex enemy attack patterns, status effects, and multi-stage boss fights are simplified to basic melee charges, single projectile firing, or simple area-of-effect zones (for boss).
-   **Meta-Progression System:** The entire meta-progression system, including currency collection, blueprints, permanent hero/equipment upgrades, and unlocking new characters, has been removed. The game focuses solely on single-run progression and player skill.
-   **PvP Mode and Daily Dungeons:** All multiplayer features (PvP) and additional daily content (Trial Tower, etc.) have been completely removed. The game is a purely single-player experience.
-   **Skill Rarity System:** While the concept mentioned varying rarities, for simplification, upgrades are presented as a flat pool of options without explicit rarity differentiation. The game ensures unique selections per choice.
-   **Visual Fidelity:** Graphics are simplified to basic geometric shapes and colors, suitable for p5.js drawing primitives, rather than detailed sprites or 3D models.",
  "implementation_notes": "The game will be implemented primarily using **p5.js** for rendering, game loop management, and basic physics (collision detection via distance checks and rectangle overlaps). Given the grid-based movement and simplified physics, Matter.js is not strictly necessary and would add complexity that may be overkill. The game canvas will be a fixed size, e.g., 800x600 pixels.

**Score Display:** The current score will be displayed in the top-right corner of the canvas. It should be a static text element (`text()`) rendered in white color, font size 24px, positioned clearly outside of the main gameplay area, typically `text('SCORE: ' + score, width - 180, 30);`.

**Level Progression Display:** The current level will be displayed in the top-left corner of the canvas. It should also be a static text element, rendered in white color, font size 24px, typically `text('LEVEL: ' + currentLevel, 20, 30);`.

**Grid System:** The game world will operate on a logical grid (e.g., 20x15 cells, with each cell being 40x40 pixels) which maps directly to the visual canvas. Player and enemy positions will be aligned to these grid cells or move smoothly between them.

**Room Management:** Each level will have an array of room layouts (either pre-defined or generated templates). When a room is cleared, the game will load the next room's layout and populate it with enemies. A game manager object will handle state transitions and overall game flow."
}
game_info.description: Navigate through 5 challenging levels of procedurally generated dungeon rooms. Move grid-by-grid to dodge enemies and projectiles. Stand still to auto-attack the nearest foe. Clear each room to earn powerful upgrades that stack throughout your run. Defeat increasingly difficult enemies including melee grunts, ranged shooters, heavy tanks, and a final boss. Survive to the end to claim victory!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys (↑↓←→): Move one grid cell in the corresponding direction
Spacebar: Confirm upgrade selection / Start game from menu / Resume from pause
Z: Pause/Unpause the game
Enter: Start the game from the title screen
Esc: Pause the game
R: Restart from game over screen (returns to title)
